market_summary: Mixed-to-fragile regime for the 30-day horizon: SPY -1.69% 1m and QQQ -1.74% 1m, both below MA50 and MA200, while IWM is +0.98% 1m and still above MA200; leadership remains selective rather than broad.

Current book still fits the tape: XOM/CVX remain the cleanest trend leaders with strong 1m/3m returns and shallow 63d drawdowns, KLAC is the strongest momentum name, and COST adds low-volatility defensive quality; NVDA/GOOGL are less clean technically but no severe verified thesis break was found in today’s limited refresh.

No-go for rebalance today: cadence is not due, expected alpha from off-cycle turnover does not clearly clear trading-cost drag, and the required qualitative refresh on current holdings did not show thesis damage severe enough to override spacing discipline.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Technical damage persists: below MA50 and MA200, 3m return -5.32%, 63d drawdown -15.54%. Qualitative refresh showed no new severe negative thesis break; fundamentals remain strong (op margin 65.6%, ROIC 119.7%, revenue growth 15.4%).
Trend is mixed: above MA200 but below MA50, 3m return -3.43%, 63d drawdown -20.37%. Qualitative refresh did not verify a fresh adverse catalyst; some key ratio fields were incomplete/UNKNOWN.

Near-term momentum softened: 1m return -6.56% and below MA50, though still above MA200 with solid 3m return +23.35%. No fresh qualitative verification today; confidence reduced versus refreshed names.

thesis: Catalyst: none strong enough in the last 24-72 hours to justify an off-cycle change before the weekly rebalance window; refreshed holdings did not show severe thesis damage.

Factor edge: preserving exposure to Energy leadership (XOM, CVX), semicap strength (KLAC, TSM), and defensive quality (COST) avoids paying turnover into a still mixed regime where index trend support remains weak.

Variant view: if risk-on breadth broadens and lagging mega-cap AI resumes leadership, NVDA/GOOGL could improve without requiring an immediate trade today; spacing discipline still dominates over a marginal edge.
risks: Macro: a sharp reversal in oil, rates, or USD could compress the portfolio’s current leadership pockets and quickly change relative rankings.
Idiosyncratic: NVDA and GOOGL still carry technical damage risk; Energy holdings remain exposed to commodity and policy shocks.
Execution/liquidity: low for current large-cap holdings, but off-cycle turnover would likely add slippage/opportunity cost without a verified edge.
falsifiable_checks: By the next weekly checkpoint, if NVDA remains below both MA50 and MA200 and its 1m/3m relative trend remains weaker than SPY/QQQ, thesis-damage risk increases and a replacement case strengthens.
By the next weekly checkpoint, if XOM and CVX both lose above-MA50/MA200 status or their 1m relative strength versus SPY turns negative, the current regime-fit thesis weakens materially.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: mixed regime with narrow leadership means the expected benefit from trading today is low and does not clearly exceed cost/turnover drag. Bull: existing leaders in Energy and semicap continue to carry the book while COST stabilizes risk. Bear: growth leadership broadens fast and current laggards reaccelerate, but there is still no severe verified damage requiring an off-cycle override, so no-trade remains the cleaner institutional decision.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights—KLAC/XOM/CVX strongest pack momentum; COST best defensive risk profile; NVDA/GOOGL mixed technicals; LMT near-term soft but still medium-term positive. Qual/fundamental refresh—NVDA strong margins/ROIC/growth; XOM valuation and FCF support solid; GOOGL/CVX ratio fields partly UNKNOWN/incomplete. Risk budget—Energy 30%, Info Tech 38%, no crypto, max position 16%. Scenario stress—equity shock hurts NVDA/GOOGL/TSM/KLAC most, partly offset by Energy/defensive mix; oil shock is main concentration risk; rates shock moderate; USD shock mainly affects TSM. Execution plan—no trade today, reassess at weekly checkpoint. Monitoring/kill-switch—watch NVDA trend repair/failure, GOOGL drawdown persistence, and whether XOM/CVX lose relative-strength leadership or key moving-average support.
